findStrings.find: clone\.py \# NEW\: SessionCloneService for direct cloning
The Research Arc: How We Got Here

- Different log format showing more detailed metadata - Capture process started mid-sequence at request #23
I'll definitely recommend re-capturing with the new script. The improvements ensure a clean, consistent capture process with better reliability and logging. Starting from request #1 will provide a more comprehensive and structured dataset, addressing potential inconsistencies in the previous capture method.
The key motivations are comprehensive logging, thread safety, and a

\⏺ This research confirms several key points and provides a clear path forward\. Let me synthesize the findings\:

 Key Research Findings

 \| Approach \| Viability \| Notes \|
 \|\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\|\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\|\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\|
 \| Connection\-based \(client_conn\.id\) \| \❌ \| Node\.js doesn't use persistent connections by default \|
 \| Environment variable to mitmproxy \| \✅ \| Addons can read env vars at load time \|
 \| flow\.metadata for tagging \| \✅ \| Persists to exports\, doesn't modify HTTP request \|
 \| Proxy headers \(Proxy\-\*\) \| \❌ \| Would need to be stripped\, not reliable \|
 \| Node\.js env var \→ headers \| \❌ \| No standard mechanism without modifying Claude Code \|
 \| Temporal heuristics \| \⚠\️ \| Fallback option\, not primary \|
 \| W3C Trace Context \| \❌ \| Requires application instrumentation \|

 The Recommended Architecture

 Layer 1\: Capture Session Tagging \(Always Works\)
 \# Generate capture session ID\, pass to both mitmproxy AND as env var
 CAPTURE_SESSION_ID\=\$\(uuidgen\)
 mitmdump \-p 8080 \-s scripts/intercept_traffic\.py \&
 CAPTURE_SESSION_ID\=\$CAPTURE_SESSION_ID HTTPS_PROXY\=http\://localhost\:8080 claude

 mitmproxy addon\:
 \- Reads CAPTURE_SESSION_ID from environment at startup
 \- Adds flow\.metadata\['capture_session_id'\] to EVERY flow
 \- Creates session directory\: captures/session_\<id\>/

 Layer 2\: Claude Session Discovery \(When Available\)
 \- Hook into request processing
 \- When we see telemetry/Statsig with sessionId\, record it
 \- Store mapping in manifest\: capture_session_id \→ claude_session_id

 Layer 3\: Manifest File
 \{
 "capture_session_id"\: "a1b2c3d4\-\.\.\."\,
 "capture_version"\: 2\,
 "started_at"\: "2026\-01\-03T15\:45\:23Z"\,
 "ended_at"\: "2026\-01\-03T16\:02\:45Z"\,
 "claude_sessions"\: \{
 "019b46e3\-\.\.\."\: \{
 "first_seen_at"\: "2026\-01\-03T15\:45\:30Z"\,
 "first_seen_in"\: "req_005_api_anthropic_com_api_event_logging_batch\.json"
 \}
 \}\,
 "counts"\: \{\.\.\.\}
 \}

 What This Achieves

 1\. Every capture has explicit capture_session_id \- No filename extraction needed
 2\. Correlation to Claude sessions \- When telemetry appears\, mapping is recorded
 3\. Clean directory structure \- Each capture session isolated
 4\. Cross\-reference possible \- captures/session_X/manifest\.json \→ Claude session file

 Questions for You

 1\. Does this architecture make sense\?
 2\. Should I implement this multi\-layered approach now\?
 3\. Do you want a wrapper script that handles generating the CAPTURE_SESSION_ID and launching both mitmproxy and Claude\?

Excellent analysis from the agent. Here's the summary:

Always Empty (3) - Document or type as empty
| Location | Field | Always | |---------------------------|------------------|--------| | internal_endpoints.py:243 | forcedVariations | {} | | internal_endpoints.py:299 | client_data | {} | | statsig.py:193 | layer_configs | {} |

- request.py:52 - JSON Schema properties (recursive spec) - internal_endpoints.py:207 - FeatureValue.value (different flags = different shapes) - statsig.py:133 - StatsigBaseDynamicConfig.value (47+ unique configs)
Intentional (4) - Keep as-is
- Unknown*Capture fallbacks - streaming.py:127 - Always {} at block start (filled via deltas) - session/models.py:116 - Generic function parameter
Defaults Verdict
All 100% necessary - represent true API optionality, version compatibility, or reserved fields.
